original: It looks like you’re trying to solve
This is an Emitter node, and your task starts here. Emitter nodes provide power to the circuit system in the form of energy beams, and it’s gonna be your job to direct these beams around...

Goal nodes, like this one here, are ultimately where you’re going to need to make the beams end up. Fully powering every goal node in the circuit by providing a beam to every opening will override the circuit system and give you control over whatever the circuit is attached to.

Dynamic nodes are the only way you can interact with the circuit directly. Every time you interact with one it’ll rotate 90 degrees clockwise, and in the process, direct any beams connected to an opening out of all of the other open directions.

You’re not going to be able to completely control the circuit system though. Many of the nodes are going to resist your attempts to modify them, like these Locked nodes. You won’t be able to rotate them, but you will still be able to pass beams through them, and just like dynamic nodes, they will split beams out of all of their openings whenever you power them.

Some nodes in the circuit system are just blank. These spaces will allow beams to pass through them, but they won’t split a beam that passes in on one side out of the rest, it will simply transition straight through.

A further complication are little traps that the circuit system designers may have left in your way. These Hazard nodes will immediately overload the circuit if they ever receive power, so be sure to never hit them with a beam!

Some circuit systems grew so complex that the circuit designers needed to invent whole new ways to route their logic around the systems they designed, like these Teleport nodes. Every teleport node is a member of a pair, and powering one side will also power the paired one too! You can tell which node is paired thanks to the little symbol tucked in the corner of the node.
Speaking of complexity, you may find these specially-marked locked nodes. These nodes are actually locked in place temporarily, and if you can find...

...an Override node, you can unlock them! Leading an energy beam into an override node will convert all temporarily-locked nodes into dynamic nodes. An override node may have more than one opening, but it only needs one beam current to activate its special ability.

I’ve given you all the help I can for now, so it’s time I got out of your way and let you try to put what I’ve told you to use and override a circuit system!

Good luck!
